narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout

watchFor: Hyperscaler capex cuts at next earnings
NVDA revenue growth slows below 20%
China export controls expand to HBM

Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant narrative, driving strong performance in semiconductors and related hardware. Software is also seeing a boost from AI adoption, though some sub-sectors face valuation concerns.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
10Y yield breaks above 5% — kills the duration bid that powers software multiples
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — would hit key chipmakers

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer spending remains resilient in some areas like discount stores and online marketplaces, but discretionary categories like apparel and travel show mixed signals. Auto manufacturers are navigating a complex market.
watchFor: Unexpectedly weak retail sales data (ex-auto) for May/June
Consumer confidence index drops significantly below 90
Increase in credit card delinquencies beyond current trends

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and entertainment companies are seeing mixed results, with some benefiting from AI integration and new product launches, while others face valuation concerns or competition.
watchFor: Major tech companies report slowing ad revenue growth
New regulations on social media or streaming content
Significant shift in consumer media consumption habits

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense remains a strong sector driven by geopolitical tensions and government spending. Industrial machinery shows strength from growth drivers, while logistics faces mixed signals.
watchFor: Significant de-escalation of geopolitical conflicts
Unexpected cuts in defense budgets from major nations
Global trade volumes decline sharply

Healthcare.thesis: GLP-1 drugs continue to drive significant growth for leading pharmaceutical companies, while other areas like medical devices face competitive pressures.
watchFor: New clinical trial data for GLP-1 drugs show unexpected side effects or reduced efficacy
Increased regulatory scrutiny or pricing pressure on GLP-1 medications
Major healthcare policy changes impacting drug manufacturers or insurers

Financials.thesis: The financial sector is generally strong, with diversified banks and credit services showing positive momentum from improved trading revenues and consumer spending. Asset management also benefits from market strength.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed, compressing NIMs
Significant increase in loan defaults across consumer or commercial segments
Major regulatory changes impacting bank profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The consumer defensive sector, particularly in household and personal products, is showing strength from strategic initiatives and positive market momentum.
watchFor: Unexpected slowdown in consumer staples spending
Increased competition from private label brands
Raw material costs rise significantly, impacting margins

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector is seeing mixed signals, with some benefiting from AI-driven energy demand while others face broader market uncertainties.
watchFor: Significant changes in energy policy or regulations
Unexpected fluctuations in natural gas or electricity prices
Major outages or infrastructure failures

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is facing mixed signals, with some companies experiencing declines despite revenue surges, indicating valuation concerns and geopolitical factors at play.
watchFor: Global industrial production slows down significantly
New tariffs or trade restrictions on raw materials
Major supply disruptions in key mining regions

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is seeing renewed interest as a safe-haven asset and inflation hedge, while silver faces demand weakness despite its industrial applications.
watchFor: Fed signals aggressive rate hikes, strengthening the dollar
Significant de-escalation of global geopolitical tensions
Unexpectedly low inflation prints
